name: Bark (Generative Text-to-Audio)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Suno AI; model weights on HuggingFace

performance.notes: Slow — generative model; ~5-20 seconds per sentence even on GPU; small model available but lower quality

agent_readiness.notes: Suno's text-to-audio model generating not just speech but also music, sound effects, and laughter. Highly expressive: can produce [laughs], [sighs], [music] etc. from text prompts. Speaker presets and custom voice prompts (voice cloning from audio). Supports multiple languages. Simple API: generate_audio(text, history_prompt). Very slow even on GPU — not for real-time TTS. Better for audio content creation than live applications.

gotchas: Very slow — 5-60 seconds per generation on GPU
Non-deterministic outputs — same input produces different audio
Development appears less active since Suno v2/v3 cloud models
best_when: Expressive audio content generation with laughter/music/effects
Voice cloning for content creation (with consent)
Offline TTS not requiring real-time response
avoid_when: Real-time TTS applications (use Piper or ElevenLabs)
Production voice assistants requiring low latency
summary: Bark by Suno is a generative text-to-audio model producing expressive speech with laughter, sighs, music, and sound effects from text prompts. Speaker presets and voice cloning. Supports multiple languages. Very slow (5-60s per sentence) — not for real-time use. Best for offline content creation requiring highly expressive, non-deterministic audio generation. Large 9GB+ model. Less active since Suno focused on cloud v3.
